highlights: Richmond opened the best-of-nine BCJHL championship series with a 7-0 rout of the Kelowna Packers at Richmond Arena. Kelowna had posted a 45-5-2 regular-season record; Richmond coach Orland Kurtenbach noted 'we know it's not going to be this easy.'

Frank Romeo stopped 27 shots for the shutout against the team that had released him the previous off-season; he called it 'sweet revenge.' Packers coach Bill Hogaboam: 'They outhustled us all over the ice ... we played horrible and everybody on our team knows it.'
Dave Tomlinson led the Sockeye offence with two goals; Jason Phillips, Bill Hardy, Mike Claringbull, Mike McCormick and Steve Jaques added singles.
Source: Vancouver Sun Mar 18 1987 p.26, image 495098412.

Richmond took a 2-0 series lead with a 5-4 overtime win at Richmond Arena. Defenceman Matt Hervey scored at 3:37 of the first overtime, pouncing on a Dean Rutledge rebound after Rutledge and Paul Rutherford broke in on a two-on-one against Kelowna netminder Dusty Imoo.

The game was tied 2-2 after one and 4-3 Richmond after two. Other Sockeye goals came from Rutledge, Rutherford, Bill Hardy and Dave Tomlinson; Jamie Kurki, Pat Mangold, Kelly Hurd and Mike Murinko replied for Kelowna. The Sockeyes moved to 4-for-4 in overtime during the 1987 playoffs.
Source: Vancouver Sun Mar 19 1987 p.38 (image 495099771) and p.37 photo (image 495099766).

Richmond jumped to a 4-0 first-period lead en route to a 7-5 win at Kelowna, taking a 3-0 series lead. Coach Orland Kurtenbach: 'The key to any hockey game is if you've got people who are intense and taking the body.'
Dean Rutledge, Dave Tomlinson and Jason Phillips each scored twice for Richmond; Mike McCormick added a single. Kelowna was led by Jason Ruff and Pat Mangold (two apiece) and Jamie Kurki.
Frank Romeo made 26 saves for Richmond; Kelowna's Dusty Imoo and Lucien Carignan combined to stop 26 Sockeye shots. Matt Hervey suffered a shoulder separation in this game.
Source: Vancouver Sun Mar 21 1987 p.24, image 495156035; Vancouver Sun Mar 25 1987 p.20 (Hervey injury).
Richmond built a commanding 4-0 series lead with a 5-1 win at Kelowna. Three unanswered goals in the last half of the third period put the game away.

Jason Phillips opened the scoring with a shorthanded goal in the first period. Dave Tomlinson made it 2-0 early in the second before Brent Mowery scored Kelowna's only goal at 13:23 of the second. Third-period insurance came from Phillips (his second), Bill Hardy and Mike McCormick.
Source: Vancouver Sun Mar 23 1987 p.15 (image 495156572) and p.10 (image 495156700).

Richmond completed a five-game sweep of the best-of-nine BCJHL final with a 3-2 win at Richmond Arena before more than 1,100 fans, clinching the Sockeyes' first BCJHL championship in eight years and extending their post-season winning streak to 13 games.

Kelowna's Greg Andrusao opened the scoring; Bill Hardy replied for Richmond. Dean Rutledge scored the only goal of the second period, shorthanded, when his shot deflected off Packer Bruce Bianchini past Dusty Imoo to put Richmond up 2-1 at 4:26. Pat Mangold tied it 2-2 at 1:37 of the third on a power play.

Jason Phillips scored the winner at 9:31 of the third, flipping a rebound past Imoo after Phillips' initial deflection of a Steve Jaques point shot was stopped. Phillips credited Brian Kozak and Bill Hardy for creating the loose puck.

Richmond outshot Kelowna 35-24. Frank Romeo was outstanding in goal while fighting sinusitis. Phillips: 'As long as he keeps standing on his head, I think we can go all the way.' Captain Trevor Dickie hoisted the BCJHL trophy with Dean Rutledge and Rob Sumner.
Source: Vancouver Sun Mar 25 1987 p.63 (image 495157739) and p.25 (image 495157862); Province Mar 25 1987 p.20 (image 502054045).
Richmond opened the best-of-three Mowat Cup (BC Tier II provincial junior final) with a 4-3 win over the Peace-Cariboo Junior Hockey League champion Quesnel Millionaires at Richmond Arena.
Source: Times Colonist Apr 4 1987 p.32, image 508629153.

Richmond clinched the Mowat Cup with a 5-1 win over Quesnel at Richmond Arena, sweeping the best-of-three series two games to none — the Sockeyes' fourth consecutive series sweep of the 1987 playoffs and their 15th straight playoff win.
Goal scorers documented in the Quesnel Cariboo Observer box score include Mike Claringbull (assisted by Jason Phillips and Brian Kozak) and Jason Phillips (assisted by Bill Hardy and Stan Czenczek).
Richmond advanced to the Doyle Cup (Western Canadian semifinal) vs. the Red Deer Rustlers, beginning Apr 7 at Richmond Arena.
Source: Vancouver Sun Apr 6 1987 p.18 (image 495086278) and p.11 (image 495086548); Quesnel Cariboo Observer Apr 8 1987 p.C1 box score (image 688061732) and p.B4 retrospective (image 688061731).

Richmond opened the Western Canada Centennial Cup semifinal (Doyle Cup) with an 11-4 rout of the Red Deer Rustlers at the Richmond Arena in front of about 1,250 fans — the Sockeyes' 16th consecutive post-season win.

Dave Tomlinson opened the scoring 21 seconds in; defenceman Matt Hervey doubled the lead moments later. Red Deer's Kevan Melrose and Greg Pulliam replied on the power play to make it 4-2 after the first period. Rich Anderson narrowed it to 4-3 early in the second before Richmond pulled away.
Richmond outshot Red Deer 60-34. Frank Romeo started in goal for Richmond; Glen Furgason started for Red Deer.
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 8 1987, p.21, image 558334501.

Richmond rallied from 4-2 down after two periods to beat Red Deer 6-4 at the Richmond Arena and take a 2-0 series lead. Attendance 800 (per official box). The Sockeyes' 17th consecutive post-season win.

Goals — 1st: Red Deer, Ward 9:13; Richmond, Hervey (Czenczek) 18:55 PP. 2nd: Red Deer, Ward (deGraaf, Linnell) 6:06 PP; Richmond, Jaques 14:46; Red Deer, Gosselin (Linnell, Wiltshire) 17:18 PP; Red Deer, Pulliam (Melrose) 19:54 SH. 3rd: Hervey (Tomlinson, Czenczek) 5:42 PP; Rutledge (Rutherford, Tomlinson) 9:52 PP; Hervey (Rutherford, Tomlinson) 11:19 PP; Kozak (McCormick, Hervey) 12:47 PP.

The swing came at 6:55 of the third when Red Deer defenceman Keith Lightbown drew a major + game misconduct for pummelling Dave Tomlinson (who never dropped his gloves). Richmond scored four on the ensuing five-minute major: Hervey completed his hat trick and got the winner; Rutledge tied it 4-4; Kozak added insurance 88 seconds after the winner.
Shots on goal: Richmond 52, Red Deer 30. Goaltenders: Romeo (Richmond), Weiss (Red Deer — pickup from Sherwood Park Crusaders, first start in place of Furgason).
Post-game, Red Deer forwards Rich Anderson (gross misconduct) and Brad Wilson (match penalty) were involved in an altercation with Richmond fans after climbing into the stands.
Source: Red Deer Advocate Apr 9 1987 p.1, p.11, p.14 (images 558334781, 558334839, 558334897).
Red Deer beat Richmond 6-3 at the Red Deer Arena before 1,628 fans to cut the series lead to 2-1. Richmond led 2-0 after the first period but were outscored 5-0 in the second.

Goals — 1st: McCormick (Dickie, Czenczek) 1:23; Hardy (Kozak) ~16:28 PP. 2nd: Red Deer, Georgsen (Pulliam, Hakstol) 2:58 PP; deGraaf (Wiltshire) 7:13 PP; Wiltshire (deGraaf) 7:39 PP; Ward (Hakstol) 15:54; Duchak (Gosselin, Hakstol) 17:02. 3rd: Red Deer, Gosselin (Ward, Wiltshire) 1:09; Tomlinson 9:35 SH (unassisted).
Three of Red Deer's first five goals came on the power play. Shots on goal: Richmond 40, Red Deer 31. Goaltenders: Romeo (Richmond), Weiss (Red Deer, 'magnificent performance' per RDA).
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 13 1987, p.7 and p.9 (images 558335628, 558335639).

Red Deer won 7-1 at the Red Deer Arena before 1,652 fans to tie the best-of-seven series 2-2. The chippy contest featured 392 penalty minutes (221 to Richmond) and a 30-minute second-period delay caused by four on-ice fights and a fracas behind the Richmond bench instigated by fans throwing debris including eggs.

Goals — 1st: Puhalsky (Pulliam) 1:56; Georgsen (Melrose) 8:45 PP; Anderson (Pulliam, Wiltshire) 16:09 PP. 2nd: Ward (Duchak, deGraaf) 12:11; Duchak (Wiltshire) 16:17 PP; Anderson (Duchak) 17:36 PP. 3rd: Richmond — Tomlinson (Bobbitt) 1:39; Ward (Duchak) 15:21.
Red Deer scored four goals on the power play. Shots on goal: Red Deer 46, Richmond 34. Goaltenders: Stewart (Richmond — his first start of the series in place of Romeo), Weiss (Red Deer).
The CAHA warned that continuing fan misconduct could force the series to be awarded, cancelled, or moved to a neutral site.
Source: Red Deer Advocate Apr 13 1987 p.1, p.7, p.9 (images 558335595, 558335628, 558335639).

Red Deer beat Richmond 7-5 in 10-minute overtime at the Red Deer Arena before 1,672 fans to take a 3-2 series lead. Dixon Ward (Pulliam, Duchak) scored the winner 59 seconds into OT; Geoff Gosselin added an empty-net marker at 9:50, 14 seconds after Jason Phillips was stopped on a penalty shot with 24 seconds remaining.

Goals — 1st: Anderson (Gosselin, Duchak) 7:14; Kozak 9:42; Pulliam (Ward, Duchak) 9:34 PP; Puhalsky (Hakstol) 12:08; Kozak (Jaques, Czenczek) 18:38 PP. 2nd: Anderson (Wiltshire) 8:40 PP; Rutherford (Kozak) 12:24 PP. 3rd: Claringbull 5:08 PP; Phillips (Dickie, Clarke) 11:43; Puhalsky (Duchak, Lebsack) 13:16. OT: Ward (Pulliam, Duchak) 0:59; Gosselin (Melrose, Linnell) 9:50 EN.

Richmond goalie change: Romeo started; Jamie Stewart relieved him at 8:40 of the second period after Anderson's power-play goal made it 4-3 Red Deer. Stewart played the rest including all of overtime.

Shots on goal: Richmond 51 (16-16-12-7), Red Deer 45 (15-11-11-8). Goaltenders: Romeo/Stewart (Richmond), Weiss (Red Deer — 46 saves). Referee Cliff Stauble awarded the Phillips penalty shot after ruling a Rustler had intentionally dislodged the net; Weiss stopped it with his pad.
With the CAHA monitoring, the home crowd was notably better-behaved.
Source: Red Deer Advocate Apr 15 1987 p.21 and p.23 (images 558335948, 558335956); Vancouver Sun Apr 15 1987 p.15 (image 495194273).
Richmond beat Red Deer 7-3 at the Red Deer Arena (attendance 1,732 per official box; RDA narrative reports 2,020) to force a deciding seventh game. Richmond led 5-1 after the first period.

Goals — 1st: Clarke (Hervey) 7:38 PP; Bobbitt 9:19; Red Deer, deGraaf (Puhalsky) 10:46 PP; Tomlinson (Jaques) 16:22; Clarke (Czenczek) 19:13 PP; Clarke (Hervey, Czenczek) 19:29 PP. 2nd: Red Deer, Ward (Lebsack) 8:55; deGraaf (Duchak, Puhalsky) 18:04. 3rd: Kozak (Hervey, Phillips) 5:32; McCormick (Gunn, Moller) 17:32.
Rob Clarke recorded a power-play hat trick in the first period (7:38, 19:13, 19:29). Kozak's third-period goal at 5:32 came during a Richmond penalty and blunted a Rustler push.

Shots on goal: Richmond 36 (16-6-14), Red Deer 33 (7-14-12). Goaltenders: Stewart (Richmond, went the distance); Weiss started for Red Deer and was pulled after the first period (16 shots, 11 saves); Furgason finished.
Source: Red Deer Advocate Apr 16 1987 p.11 and p.15 (images 558336101, 558336127); Vancouver Sun Apr 16 1987 p.83 (image 495194669); Times Colonist Apr 16 1987 p.3 (image 508874627).
Richmond won the Doyle Cup with a 9-1 victory at the Red Deer Arena in the seventh and deciding game, earning the right to meet Humboldt in the Abbott Cup final.

After a scoreless first period, Richmond scored five unanswered goals in the second and added four more in the third. Pete deGraaf spoiled goaltender Jamie Stewart's shutout bid at 13:14 of the third, deflecting a Greg Pulliam point shot on a Red Deer power play.
Richmond scorers: Dave Tomlinson (hat trick), Rob Clarke, Mike Claringbull, Jason Phillips, Jim Gunn, Mike McCormick, Tony Bobbitt — 9 goals, 7 scorers. Shots on goal: Richmond 39, Red Deer 34.

Jamie Stewart went the distance in goal for Richmond (33 saves). Rustler coach Larry Billows said his team — playing their 27th playoff game in 47 days — simply ran out of gas. Kurtenbach said the Sockeyes were the better club in the final three games.

Source: Red Deer Advocate Apr 18 1987 p.1 and p.12 (images 558336223, 558336261); Calgary Herald Apr 18 1987 p.73 (image 483730219); Edmonton Journal Apr 18 1987 p.48 (image 473040181); Star-Phoenix Apr 18 1987 p.44 (image 512309668); Leader-Post Apr 18 1987 p.28 (image 496462357).

Humboldt opened the best-of-seven Abbott Cup (Western Canadian Junior A final) with a 5-3 overtime win at the Humboldt Uniplex. Richmond led 2-1 after the second period; teams tied 3-3 after regulation.

Curtis Chamberlin scored three goals for Humboldt, including the overtime winner on a two-on-one pass from Joey Johannson, firing high over a sliding Frank Romeo with 1:39 remaining in the 10-minute OT. Brad Bergen (McDougall, 0:26 2nd PP) and Bill McDougall (time not captured) had the other Humboldt goals.
Bryon Moller (1st), Matt Hervey (Claringbull, 1:17 2nd PP) and Jason Phillips (Czenczek, Clarke, 8:02 3rd PP) scored for Richmond.
Source: Star-Phoenix Apr 21 1987 p.41 (image 512310223) and p.43 box (image 512310237); Vancouver Sun Apr 21 1987 p.28 (image 495241884).
Richmond evened the series 1-1 with a 5-3 win at the Humboldt Uniplex.

Goals — 1st: Czenczek (Hervey) 1:54 PP; Humboldt, Shyiak (box lists 'Nelson') 8:22 PP; Phillips (Clarke, Jaques) 8:22; Humboldt, Luke (McDougall, Wingate) 16:46 PP. 2nd: Phillips (Hervey, Czenczek) 10:37 PP; Kozak (Hervey, Phillips) 17:37 PP. 3rd: Humboldt, McDougall (Bergen) 10:45; Phillips 18:05 unassisted.

Jason Phillips recorded a hat trick; Stan Czenczek and Brian Kozak had the others. Power plays: Richmond 3/5, Humboldt 2/8. Shots on goal: Humboldt 45, Richmond 31. Goaltenders: Romeo (Richmond), Backlund (Humboldt).
Source: Leader-Post Apr 22 1987 p.18 (image 496462581); Star-Phoenix Apr 22 1987 p.36 (image 512310501) and p.38 box (image 512310521); Vancouver Sun Apr 22 1987 p.60 (image 495243576).
Tony Bobbitt (McCormick, Czenczek) scored 1:04 into the 10-minute overtime to give Richmond a 4-3 win at the Humboldt Uniplex in front of fewer than 900 fans. The Sockeyes took a 2-1 series lead.

Scoreless first period. 2nd: Humboldt, McDougall (Shyiak, Rice) 7:14; Tomlinson (Rutledge, Czenczek) 13:42 SH. 3rd: Claringbull (Hervey, Tomlinson) 4:44; Humboldt, Chamberlin (McDougall, Wingate) 9:08; Clarke (Kozak, Tomlinson) 19:27 (tying goal with 33 seconds remaining). OT: Bobbitt (McCormick, Czenczek) 1:04.

Frank Romeo was named the story of the game with 36 saves, including several would-be goals off Chamberlin's stick. Humboldt outshot Richmond 39-23. Richmond took 13 of 25 minors and the only major, giving Humboldt 9 power plays — Broncos went 0/9. Jim Gunn drew a 2nd-period high-sticking major at 9:58.
Source: Star-Phoenix Apr 23 1987 p.14 (image 512310808) and p.16 box (image 512310816); Leader-Post Apr 23 1987 p.14 (image 496462652); Vancouver Sun Apr 23 1987 p.61 (image 495246503).
Humboldt won 2-1 at Richmond Arena on Saturday night to even the best-of-seven series at 2-2. It was Richmond's first home game since April 8.
Bill McDougall and Brad Bergen scored for Humboldt. Dean Rutledge replied with Richmond's lone goal in the third period.
Source: Calgary Herald Apr 26 1987, p.4, image 483768383; Times Colonist Apr 26 1987, p.12, image 508877216.
Matt Hervey scored twice as Richmond beat Humboldt 5-3 at Richmond Arena to take a 3-2 series lead. Humboldt led 2-0 after the first period before Richmond tied it 2-2 in the second.
Dave Tomlinson scored the eventual winning goal. Brian Kozak and Paul Rutherford added the other Richmond goals.
Bill McDougall scored twice for Humboldt; Garnet Kazuik had the other goal. Frank Romeo made 30 saves for Richmond; Bruce Hoffort stopped 22 for Humboldt.
Source: Leader-Post Apr 27 1987, p.14, image 496463200; Star-Phoenix Apr 27 1987, p.15, image 512311911; Nanaimo Daily News Apr 27 1987, p.10, image 325076956.

Humboldt forced a seventh and deciding game with a 4-3 overtime win at Richmond Arena. Mike Gaber deflected home a Brad Bergen point shot at 3:31 of the 10-minute full-time overtime. Richmond led 1-0 after one and were tied 2-2 after two; Tomlinson put Richmond ahead 3-2 at 7:44 of the third.
Paul Rutherford scored twice for Richmond; Dave Tomlinson had the other Sockeye goal. Bill McDougall (2) and Curtis Chamberlin also scored for Humboldt. Humboldt outshot Richmond 46-45.

Two Richmond mistakes proved costly: Tony Bobbitt took a delay-of-game minor for closing his hand on the puck at 8:28 of the third, and Humboldt tied the game 3-3 on the ensuing power play. Then at 3:02 of overtime, defenceman Steve Jaques received a five-minute match penalty for helmet-butting Bill McDougall; McDougall received a minor for interference, and Gaber's winner came at 4-on-4 with Humboldt keeping pressure on during the subsequent power play.
Goaltender Bruce Hoffort made 42 saves for Humboldt.
Source: Nanaimo Daily News Apr 29 1987, p.9, image 325077439; Vancouver Sun Apr 29 1987, p.25, image 495257020; Alberni Valley Times Apr 29 1987, p.7, image 560108301.

Dave Tomlinson scored with 2:37 remaining in regulation — 35 seconds after Humboldt had tied the game 4-4 at 16:48 of the third period — to give Richmond a 5-4 win at Richmond Arena and the Abbott Cup, the Western Canadian Junior A championship, four games to three.
Richmond led 3-1 after the first period and took a 4-3 edge into the third. Matt Hervey, Dean Rutledge, Jason Phillips and Bill Hardy had the other Sockeye goals.

Brett Stewart, Kevin Luke, Al Novakowski and Curtis Chamberlin scored for Humboldt. The Broncos outshot Richmond 40-35. Richmond outscored Humboldt 26-24 across the seven games; each team won two one-goal games.

The Broncos declined to take part in the traditional post-game handshake at centre ice and were booed by the Richmond Arena crowd. Coach Bernie Lynch said: 'We're just sending them a message: You ain't won nothin' yet. We're going to be the national champions.'

Source: Leader-Post Apr 30 1987, p.32, image 496463470; Star-Phoenix Apr 30 1987, p.45, image 512312872; Red Deer Advocate Apr 30 1987, p.20, image 558337700; Province Apr 30 1987, p.12, image 502021976; Times Colonist Apr 30 1987, p.16, image 508878542.
Richmond opened the Centennial Cup tournament with a 7-3 win over the Dartmouth Fuel Kids at the Humboldt Uniplex on Sunday afternoon. Richmond led 3-1 after one and 6-2 after two.
Jason Phillips scored three goals; Bryon Moller, Steve Jaques, Dave Tomlinson and Matt Hervey added singles. Jerry Scott (2) and Brian King replied for Dartmouth.
Richmond outshot Dartmouth 44-29 and took 11 of 17 minors plus a major; both teams drew a misconduct.
Source: Star-Phoenix May 4 1987 (image 512093788); Red Deer Advocate May 4 1987 p.9 (image 558421571); The Province May 4 1987 p.18 (image 502027542); The Winnipeg Sun May 4 1987 (image 736882093).
Richmond defeated Pembroke 4-1 at the Humboldt Uniplex on Monday night to move to 2-0 in round-robin play. The Sockeyes scored in the first period then erupted for three third-period goals.
Goals — 1st: Kozak (Phillips, Hervey) 2:39. 3rd: Pembroke — Dupont (Eastwood, Clarke) 4:06 PP; Czenczek (Moller, Dickie) 6:35; Tomlinson (Phillips) 15:01; Tomlinson (Rutledge, Rutherford) 18:26.

Shots on goal: Richmond 26 (8-7-11), Pembroke 25 (10-6-9). Goaltenders: Romeo (Richmond), Robb (Pembroke). Power plays: Richmond 0/2; Pembroke 1/9. Richmond took nine of 13 minors from referee Wes Smith; Kurtenbach afterward: 'All I'm asking for is a fair shake from the refereeing.'
Source: Star-Phoenix May 5 1987, p.17 box score (image 512094503); The Vancouver Sun May 5 1987, p.33 (image 495157600); The Times-Transcript May 5 1987 (image 1107094171).

Host Humboldt Broncos defeated Richmond 6-1 in the round-robin finale — the first blowout in eight meetings between the two Western finalists in three weeks. Humboldt finished round-robin 3-0; Richmond 2-1.

Bill McDougall opened the scoring on a power-play rebound at 6:17 of the first. Stan Czenczek answered with a power-play slap shot past Bruce Hoffort at 17:26 to tie it. Garnet Kazuik broke the tie shorthanded at 13:20 of the second, stealing the puck from Steve Jaques at the Richmond blue line for a clear breakaway on Jamie Stewart. Humboldt led 4-1 after two.

Humboldt scorers: McDougall (2), Kazuik (2), Duncan Ryhorchuk, Curtis Chamberlin. Shots: Humboldt 37, Richmond 19. Humboldt took 15 of 27 minors; both teams had two five-minute majors and two game misconducts, plus Humboldt added two 10-minute misconducts.
Kurtenbach: 'We got outworked, outplayed, out-everythinged. The "oomph" just wasn't there. Some nights it just doesn't go, and tonight was one of them.'

Source: Star-Phoenix May 7 1987 p.17 (image 512096770); Calgary Herald May 7 1987 p.13 (image 483641750); Vancouver Sun May 7 1987 p.24 (image 495159747); Red Deer Advocate May 7 1987 p.17 (image 558421757).
Richmond whipped Pembroke 9-3 at the Humboldt Uniplex in the Centennial Cup semifinal (Thursday 8:30 PM CST) to advance to Saturday's final against host Humboldt. Nine different Sockeyes scored.

Richmond scorers: Dean Rutledge, Steve Jaques, Matt Hervey, Brian Kozak, Paul Rutherford, Jason Phillips, Tony Bobbitt, Mike McCormick, Bill Hardy. Pembroke scorers: Dave Van Hoof, George Dupont, Chris Clarke.
Richmond outshot Pembroke 44-32, led 1-0 after the first and 3-0 after the second, then added six in the third. Took 11 of 17 minors.

Source: Times Colonist May 8 1987 p.12 (image 508631348); Calgary Herald May 8 1987 p.18 (image 483667308); Red Deer Advocate May 8 1987 p.22 (image 558421854); Daily Gleaner May 8 1987 p.24 (image 1099004070).

Richmond defeated Humboldt 5-2 at the Humboldt Uniplex on Saturday night (7:30 PM CST) before a standing-room-only crowd of approximately 2,400 to win the Centennial Cup, the national Tier II Junior A championship.

Goals — 1st: Tomlinson (Rutherford, Claringbull) 16:53; Phillips (Hardy) 18:13. 2nd: Humboldt, Chamberlin (Bergen) 10:47 PP. 3rd: Rutherford (Rutledge) 8:48 (eventual winner); Phillips (Kozak) 13:28; Humboldt, Luke (Bergen, Rice) 16:31; Phillips (Kozak) 19:07 empty net.

Jason Phillips recorded a hat trick. Paul Rutherford scored the eventual winning goal. Goaltender Frank Romeo made 44 saves on 46 shots; Humboldt outshot Richmond 46-23 (16-7, 11-6, 19-10). Power plays: Richmond 0/3, Humboldt 1/5. Bruce Hoffort was in goal for Humboldt.
Frank Romeo was named the game's MVP and the tournament MVP — his third player-of-the-game award across three starts; he allowed six goals in those three games.
Jason Phillips won Most Gentlemanly Player honours (five goals, seven assists across five games, zero penalties).
All-tournament team (selected before the semifinals): C Bill McDougall (Hum), LW Jason Phillips (Rich), RW Duncan Ryhorchuk (Hum), D Rob Rice (Hum), D Matt Hervey (Rich), G Grant Robb (Pem).

Kurtenbach: 'That third goal put the crimpers on it. They haven't failed me all year. In the stress situations, they've come through like gangbusters.' Humboldt coach Bernie Lynch: 'We're 156-29 in the last two years, but couldn't win the game we wanted to win. It's bitter.'
Richmond and Humboldt played nine times in three weeks between the Abbott Cup and Centennial Cup — Richmond won five, each team scored 30 goals.

Sources: Star-Phoenix May 11 1987 p.13 (image 512098529) and p.14 box score (image 512098543); Vancouver Sun May 11 1987 p.14 (image 495230735) and p.20 (image 495229991); Times Colonist May 10 1987 p.1 (image 508633163) and p.9 (image 508632785); The Province May 10 1987 p.90 all-tournament team (image 502033093); Leader-Post May 11 1987 p.20 (image 496962458).
